examples.sentence: At the charity gala, Lisa had the chance to rub elbows with several influential politicians and business leaders.
examples.translation: At the charity gala, Lisa had the chance to mingle socially with several influential politicians and business leaders.

examples.sentence: If you want to succeed in this industry, you really need to rub elbows with the right crowd.
examples.translation: If you want to succeed in this industry, you really need to associate socially with the right crowd.

examples.sentence: He’s been rubbing elbows with celebrities ever since he started working in Hollywood.
examples.translation: He has been mingling socially with celebrities ever since he started working in Hollywood.

examples.sentence: We were rubbing elbows with some of the top executives during the conference dinner last night.
examples.translation: We were socializing with some of the top executives during the conference dinner last night.

examples.sentence: After applying the lotion, make sure to rub it in thoroughly so your skin absorbs all the moisture.
examples.translation: After applying the lotion, make sure to work it into your skin thoroughly so it absorbs all the moisture.

examples.sentence: She rubbed in the ointment gently to soothe the rash on her arm.
examples.translation: She worked the ointment into her skin gently to soothe the rash on her arm.

examples.sentence: Don’t forget to rub in the sunscreen evenly to avoid getting sunburned.
examples.translation: Remember to apply the sunscreen by rubbing it into your skin evenly to avoid sunburn.

examples.sentence: I rubbed the cream in for several minutes until it disappeared completely.
examples.translation: I worked the cream into my skin for several minutes until it was fully absorbed.

examples.sentence: To make the dough flaky, you need to rub in the butter properly before adding any liquid.
examples.translation: To make the dough flaky, you need to blend the butter into it by rubbing before adding any liquid.

examples.sentence: She rubbed in the spices thoroughly to enhance the flavor of the marinade.
examples.translation: She blended the spices well by rubbing them into the marinade to enhance its flavor.

examples.sentence: When preparing the pastry, rub in the fat until the mixture looks crumbly.
examples.translation: When preparing the pastry, blend the fat by rubbing it into the mixture until it looks crumbly.

examples.sentence: I’ll rub in the herbs now so their aroma infuses the dough evenly.
examples.translation: I will blend the herbs into the dough by rubbing so their aroma spreads evenly.

examples.sentence: After losing the match, his opponent rubbed it in by reminding him about his previous defeats.
examples.translation: After losing the match, his opponent emphasized his defeat by reminding him of past losses.

examples.sentence: She didn’t want to rub in how badly he failed the exam, but it was hard to ignore.
examples.translation: She didn’t want to emphasize his exam failure repeatedly, but it was difficult to avoid.

examples.sentence: The lawyer rubbed in the evidence to make sure the jury fully understood the defendant’s guilt.
examples.translation: The lawyer emphasized the evidence to ensure the jury clearly saw the defendant’s guilt.

examples.sentence: When I spilled coffee on my shirt, Jake couldn’t resist rubbing it in all day.
examples.translation: When I spilled coffee on my shirt, Jake repeatedly reminded me of it to make me feel worse.

examples.sentence: Please don’t rub it in that I forgot your birthday; I already feel terrible about it.
examples.translation: Please don’t repeatedly remind me that I forgot your birthday; I already feel bad.

examples.sentence: They kept rubbing it in that I was the only one who didn’t finish the project on time.
examples.translation: They repeatedly emphasized that I was the only one who missed the project deadline.

examples.sentence: After cleaning the wound, the nurse rubbed it in with antiseptic cream to prevent infection.
examples.translation: After cleaning the wound, the nurse applied antiseptic cream by rubbing it into the skin.

examples.sentence: I rubbed it in gently so the lotion would absorb faster into my dry skin.
examples.translation: I applied the lotion by rubbing it into my dry skin so it would absorb faster.

examples.sentence: She will rub it in before bedtime to soothe her aching muscles.
examples.translation: She will apply the cream by rubbing it into her muscles before going to bed.

examples.sentence: Make sure you rub it in well; otherwise, the treatment won’t be as effective.
examples.translation: Make sure you apply it thoroughly by rubbing, or the treatment won’t work as well.

examples.sentence: The paint started to rub off after the chair was moved several times across the floor.
examples.translation: The paint began to be removed from the chair as it was rubbed against the floor.

examples.sentence: If you keep wiping the surface, the label will eventually rub off completely.
examples.translation: If you continue rubbing the surface, the label will be removed entirely.

examples.sentence: After months of use, the gold plating on his watch began to rub off.
examples.translation: After months of use, the gold coating on his watch started to be removed by rubbing.

examples.sentence: Be careful not to rub off the stickers when you clean the glass panels.
examples.translation: Be careful not to remove the stickers by rubbing them while cleaning the glass.

examples.sentence: The black ink rubs off onto your hands if you’re not careful while writing.
examples.translation: The black ink transfers to your hands if you aren’t careful while writing.

examples.sentence: That red paint tends to rub off on clothes, so wear an apron when you’re painting.
examples.translation: That red paint easily transfers onto clothes, so wear an apron during painting.

examples.sentence: Be cautious because the marker ink may rub off on other papers in your bag.
examples.translation: Be careful because the marker ink can transfer onto other papers inside your bag.

examples.sentence: The dirt rubbed off on my shoes after walking through the muddy field.
examples.translation: The dirt transferred onto my shoes after I walked through the muddy field.

examples.sentence: His positive attitude started to rub off on the whole team during the project.
examples.translation: His optimistic behavior gradually influenced the whole team throughout the project.

examples.sentence: I hope that good work ethic rubs off on the new interns this summer.
examples.translation: I hope the new interns gradually acquire a good work ethic by working closely with us.

examples.sentence: The patience she shows in tough situations has rubbed off on her children over time.
examples.translation: Her calmness in difficult times has gradually influenced her children.

examples.sentence: Sometimes bad habits rub off on us without even realizing it.
examples.translation: Sometimes we acquire bad habits gradually by being around others without noticing.

examples.sentence: I’ve been working closely with Sarah, and her enthusiasm is starting to rub off on me.
examples.translation: I’ve been with Sarah a lot, and her enthusiasm is gradually influencing me.

examples.sentence: The calm demeanor of the manager rubbed off on the whole staff during the crisis.
examples.translation: The manager’s calm behavior influenced the entire staff during the crisis.

examples.sentence: Spending time with creative people can help their innovative ideas rub off on you.
examples.translation: Being around creative people can cause their innovative ideas to influence you.

examples.sentence: Be careful; the paint might rub off on your clothes if you’re not wearing gloves.
examples.translation: Be cautious; the paint can transfer onto your clothes if you don’t wear gloves.

examples.sentence: The marker ink rubbed off on the whiteboard eraser after several uses.
examples.translation: The marker ink transferred onto the whiteboard eraser after multiple uses.

examples.sentence: I noticed the logo rubbed off on my hands after handling the package for a while.
examples.translation: I saw the logo transferred onto my hands after holding the package for some time.

examples.sentence: The color from the new jeans rubbed off on the car seat during the drive.
examples.translation: The color from the new jeans transferred to the car seat while driving.

examples.sentence: Her kindness really rubbed off on the whole group, making everyone more considerate.
examples.translation: Her kindness influenced the entire group, making everyone more thoughtful.

examples.sentence: I hope the discipline of our coach will rub off on the players before the tournament.
examples.translation: I hope the coach’s discipline will influence the players before the competition.

examples.sentence: Spending time with dedicated volunteers made their passion rub off on me too.
examples.translation: Being with committed volunteers caused their enthusiasm to influence me as well.

examples.sentence: On cold mornings, I rub my hands together to warm them up before heading out.
examples.translation: On cold mornings, I move my palms against each other to warm my hands before leaving.

examples.sentence: She was rubbing her hands together vigorously after coming inside from the snow.
examples.translation: She moved her palms back and forth energetically after entering from the snow.

examples.sentence: Tom rubbed his hands together as he waited anxiously for the test results.
examples.translation: Tom moved his palms against each other while waiting nervously for the test results.

examples.sentence: If you rub your hands together quickly, the friction will generate some heat.
examples.translation: If you move your palms against each other rapidly, the friction will produce heat.

examples.sentence: The businessman rubbed his hands together with a sly grin, anticipating the lucrative deal.
examples.translation: The businessman showed eager pleasure by rubbing his hands together, expecting a profitable deal.

examples.sentence: When she heard about the promotion, she rubbed her hands together in excitement.
examples.translation: She displayed eager anticipation by rubbing her hands together after learning of the promotion.

examples.sentence: You could tell he was rubbing his hands together at the thought of winning the contest.
examples.translation: It was obvious he was showing eager satisfaction by rubbing his hands together thinking of winning.

examples.sentence: After breaking up, his friends kept rubbing salt in his wounds by teasing him about it.
examples.translation: After the breakup, his friends made his pain worse by repeatedly reminding him about it.

examples.sentence: She felt like her teacher was rubbing salt in her wounds by pointing out her low grade in class.
examples.translation: She felt the teacher worsened her embarrassment by emphasizing her low grade.

examples.sentence: Don’t rub salt in his wounds by bringing up the mistake he already regrets.
examples.translation: Don’t make him feel worse by reminding him of the error he already feels bad about.

examples.sentence: The medic accidentally rubbed salt in the wound while trying to clean it, causing the patient to wince.
examples.translation: The medic unintentionally worsened the pain by applying salt to the wound during cleaning.

examples.sentence: Salt was rubbed into the wound to prevent infection, though it caused sharp pain initially.
examples.translation: Salt was applied to the wound to stop infection, but it caused intense pain at first.

examples.sentence: They rubbed salt in the soldier’s wound as part of the old battlefield treatment method.
examples.translation: They applied salt to the soldier’s injury according to an old treatment practice.

examples.sentence: Be careful not to rub salt in the wound when cleaning such injuries.
examples.translation: Be cautious not to worsen the pain by applying salt when treating those injuries.

examples.sentence: The rival team rubbed salt in their wounds by celebrating loudly after the final whistle.
examples.translation: The rival team worsened their emotional pain by celebrating loudly after the game ended.

examples.sentence: His classmates rubbed salt in his wounds by repeating the embarrassing story over and over.
examples.translation: His classmates aggravated his embarrassment by repeatedly telling the humiliating story.

examples.sentence: Don’t rub salt in her wounds; she’s already upset about the criticism she received.
examples.translation: Don’t make her feel worse by reminding her of the criticism she already feels bad about.

examples.sentence: When I spilled coffee on my report, my boss rubbed salt in the wound by pointing it out to everyone.
examples.translation: After I spilled coffee on my report, my boss made the situation worse by highlighting it publicly.

examples.sentence: It really rubbed salt in the wound when she reminded me of my failed audition right before the show.
examples.translation: She worsened my embarrassment by reminding me of my failed audition just before the show.

examples.sentence: Losing the championship was bad enough, but the opposing coach’s comments rubbed salt in the wound.
examples.translation: Losing the championship was painful, and the opposing coach’s remarks made it even worse.

examples.sentence: They rubbed salt in the wound to help disinfect the cut, even though it caused a sharp sting.
examples.translation: Salt was applied to the wound to disinfect it, though it caused painful stinging.

examples.sentence: The nurse carefully rubbed salt in the wound as part of the treatment procedure.
examples.translation: The nurse applied salt to the wound carefully during the treatment process.

examples.sentence: Be cautious when you rub salt in the wound because it can intensify the pain.
examples.translation: Be careful applying salt to the wound since it may increase the pain.

examples.sentence: Historically, sailors would rub salt in wounds to prevent infection, despite the agony it caused.
examples.translation: In the past, sailors applied salt to wounds to avoid infection, even though it caused great pain.

examples.sentence: His constant teasing rubbed salt in the wound after I told him about my job loss.
examples.translation: His repeated teasing made me feel worse after I shared my job loss.

examples.sentence: Don’t rub salt in the wound by reminding her about the argument last week.
examples.translation: Don’t make her feel worse by bringing up the fight from last week.

examples.sentence: The sarcastic remarks rubbed salt in the wound when she was already feeling vulnerable.
examples.translation: The sarcastic comments worsened her feelings when she was already fragile.

examples.sentence: At the networking event, I was able to rub shoulders with several industry leaders.
examples.translation: At the networking event, I mingled socially with several important industry figures.

examples.sentence: She often rubs shoulders with prominent artists at gallery openings downtown.
examples.translation: She frequently associates with well-known artists at gallery events downtown.

examples.sentence: We hope to rub shoulders with influential politicians during the conference next week.
examples.translation: We aim to socialize with powerful politicians at the upcoming conference.

examples.sentence: By volunteering at the charity, he managed to rub shoulders with some wealthy donors.
examples.translation: By volunteering, he mingled socially with some affluent donors.

examples.sentence: In his reckless youth, he rubbed shoulders with some unsavory characters that later caused trouble.
examples.translation: In his wild younger days, he spent time with some disreputable people who later brought problems.

examples.sentence: You don’t want to rub shoulders with those scammers if you want to keep your reputation clean.
examples.translation: You shouldn’t associate with those fraudsters if you want to maintain a good reputation.

examples.sentence: She reluctantly rubbed shoulders with the controversial group during her investigation.
examples.translation: She was forced to associate closely with the questionable group while investigating.

examples.sentence: That new coworker really rubs me the wrong way with her constant criticism.
examples.translation: That new coworker annoys me by frequently criticizing.

examples.sentence: I don’t mean to rub you the wrong way, but your attitude is making the team uncomfortable.
examples.translation: I don’t want to offend you, but your behavior is causing discomfort among the team.

examples.sentence: His way of speaking tends to rub people the wrong way, even though he means well.
examples.translation: His manner of talking often annoys others, although his intentions are good.

examples.sentence: Sometimes, small habits can rub someone the wrong way without anyone realizing it.
examples.translation: Occasionally, minor behaviors can unintentionally irritate someone.

examples.sentence: The strict headmaster rubbed the fear of God into the students to ensure they followed the rules.
examples.translation: The strict headmaster instilled deep fear in the students to make sure they obeyed.

examples.sentence: Parents sometimes have to rub the fear of God into their children to keep them from misbehaving.
examples.translation: Parents occasionally need to instill strong fear in their children to prevent bad behavior.

examples.sentence: The coach rubbed the fear of God into the team before the championship game, motivating them to focus.
examples.translation: The coach instilled deep fear in the team before the final game to motivate concentration.

examples.sentence: Teachers used to rub the fear of God into students with harsh punishments to maintain discipline.
examples.translation: Teachers formerly instilled strong fear in students through severe punishments to keep order.

examples.sentence: After the kids ignored the rules, their father rubbed the fear of God into them with a stern lecture.
examples.translation: Their father severely scolded the kids to frighten them into following the rules.

examples.sentence: The sergeant rubbed the fear of God into the recruits during their first week of training.
examples.translation: The sergeant harshly disciplined the recruits to frighten them into obedience.

examples.sentence: She rubbed the fear of God into her employees by threatening serious consequences for mistakes.
examples.translation: She severely warned her employees to scare them into avoiding errors.

examples.sentence: His constant complaining really rubs me up the wrong way during meetings.
examples.translation: His continual complaining annoys me during meetings.

examples.sentence: I don’t want to rub you up the wrong way, but your approach might not work here.
examples.translation: I don’t want to offend you, but your method may not be suitable here.

examples.sentence: Some of his remarks tend to rub people up the wrong way, even if he doesn’t mean harm.
examples.translation: His comments often irritate people unintentionally.

examples.sentence: She rubs people up the wrong way with her blunt honesty, but many appreciate her transparency.
examples.translation: Her frankness annoys some, but many value her openness.

examples.sentence: At the museum, they displayed Dorothy's iconic ruby slippers from The Wizard of Oz.
examples.translation: At the museum, they showed Dorothy’s famous red shoes from The Wizard of Oz.

examples.sentence: The ruby slippers sparkled under the glass case, captivating visitors of all ages.
examples.translation: The red shoes shone brightly under the glass, fascinating visitors young and old.

examples.sentence: Collectors often seek replicas of the ruby slippers as a piece of cinematic history.
examples.translation: Collectors frequently look for copies of the ruby slippers as valuable movie memorabilia.

examples.sentence: She wore ruby slippers to the costume party, instantly recognizable to fans of the classic film.
examples.translation: She dressed in red shoes at the party, easily identified by fans of the old movie.

examples.sentence: For many, that old family heirloom is their ruby slippers—guaranteeing a safe return no matter what.
examples.translation: For many, the old heirloom is like ruby slippers, ensuring security and return.

examples.sentence: Winning the scholarship felt like finding ruby slippers, a sure way to achieve her dreams.
examples.translation: Getting the scholarship seemed like having ruby slippers, a guaranteed path to success.

examples.sentence: The apprenticeship was his ruby slippers, opening doors to a promising career.
examples.translation: The apprenticeship was like ruby slippers, providing a guaranteed route to a good career.

examples.sentence: The phrase ‘follow your dreams’ has become the ruby slippers of popular culture—symbolizing hope and transformation.
examples.translation: The phrase ‘follow your dreams’ symbolizes hope and change like the ruby slippers in culture.

examples.sentence: For the fans, the ruby slippers represent more than shoes—they’re a symbol of nostalgia and magical change.
examples.translation: To fans, ruby slippers mean much more than footwear; they symbolize magic and nostalgia.

examples.sentence: Using that technology was her ruby slippers, providing a clear route to escape the old limitations.
examples.translation: That technology acted as her ruby slippers, offering a way out from old restrictions.
